title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on Factor in EMIs and Treatment Costs while Deciding your Critical Illness Coverage - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: critical illness cover, critical illness insurance, treatment cost, critical illness insurance policy
Body Content:
The large number of people being afflicted by critical illness is staggering, but not surprising. The era we live in is rife with rushed schedules, poor nutrition habits, and an overall hectic existence. Critical illnesses have increased by leaps and bounds in the past decade. The pollution, stress and poor sleep patterns don’t help much. Although many individuals realise the benefits of healthy regimes and precautions to maintain good health, critical illness can spring up suddenly. It is as important to remain financially secure in case this happens, as it is to try your best to be healthy in the first place. The only solution to safeguard your financial condition when severe illness befalls you is to get critical illness cover.
Critical illness cover is a special health plan that provides cost coverage for treatment and care of critical illness. Illnesses that fall within the purview of such conditions include cancer, multiple sclerosis, permanent paralysis, certain heart diseases, kidney failure that warrants dialysis, and other disease conditions.
The Rising Cost of Treatment
The treatment of critical illnesses can run up huge costs and these may go up to Rs. 30-40 lakh, or more, if you opt for treatment at famous private hospitals. New equipment and surgical methods are used in the treatment of many critical illnesses today, like robotic surgery, and these can increase your cost. Other expenses besides hospitalisation, include ongoing treatment and expenses to maintain your current lifestyle. A critical illness may put you out of action where employment is concerned for a long period. This means a loss of income which has to be made up. Critical illness insurance can be of great help to you at such times. Such an insurance plan will be instrumental in taking away some of the stress of you and your family during tough times.
While you may have a regular health insurance policy, purchasing insurance that covers critical illness is a necessity today. Irrespective of age or gender, you should buy a critical illness plan. Insurers pay policyholders a full lump sum amount (which is the sum assured by the plan), regardless of bills that are generated. The great part of a critical illness policy is that the lump sum amount is paid out to the insured as soon as a diagnosis of critical illness is made. The actual treatment cost may be lower than the payout, but this is no concern of the insurance company. The only clause laid down by such a policy is that the illness concerned should fall under the policy’s coverage.
When the insured receives the lump sum from the insurer, they may use it for treatment of the critical illness, paying off home loan EMIs, daily expenditure, and any other expenses incurred due to a loss of income due to severe illness. In any critical illness plan, there is normally a survival period amounting to a month. This means that the policyholder must survive for up to a month after the diagnosis of a critical illness is made, to benefit from the policy. Nonetheless, before you head to purchase a critical illness insurance policy, it is important to evaluate the amount you wish to have coverage for. This sum should be the amount required to account for all the possible expenses you may have to incur in the future.
Getting the Numbers Right
To find out how much insurance coverage you require, you have to make an accurate calculation of your existing family income. You should also calculate the amount of expenses you incur to maintain your current lifestyle. Ideally, it is a good idea to consider such amounts for a period of 2 to 3 years, as that is typically the time it takes for complete recovery from a serious illness. Additionally, the cost of treatment, including consultations, surgery, medication, etc, should be considered. Assuming that an individual has calculated this total cost and it amounts to Rs. 40 lakhs today, since calculations have to account for the future, rates of inflation must factor in too.
Critical illness cover must include the costs of rising inflation, so that total expenses under coverage don’t fall short of your financial requirements. For you to beat costs of inflation, your expenses should be multiplied by 1.5, which is the minimal inflation estimated in the years to come. With the costs of inflation, Rs. 40 lakhs will become Rs. 60 lakhs, which is the cover needed, according to the example above.
Choose the Right Combination
Critical illness insurance can be opted for as additional coverage with your present health insurance package. Furthermore, you can also buy it with a term insurance plan. When you do your homework, you will see that each of these combinations serves a different purpose, and depending on your requirements, you can opt for the coverage you need. Whatever you decide whether to go in for a standalone policy with a base health insurance plan, the right combination should give you as extensive a coverage as you require.
Conclusion
Unfortunately, many Indians don’t choose to have health insurance as they fear the high premiums involved. However, health insurance is essential and the only way you can tackle the huge costs of medical treatment. Nowadays, critical illness insurance is a must-have plan along with your basic health insurance policy. Consequently, you remain stress-free as the potential financial worry is lifted off your shoulders, and you get the best healthcare.
full_pdf_text:
Factor in EMIs and Treatment Costs while Deciding your Critical Illness Coverage Keywords: critical illness cover, critical illness insurance, treatment cost, critical illness insurance policy The large number of people being afflicted by critical illness is staggering, but not surprising. The era we live in is rife with rushed schedules, poor nutrition habits, and an overall hectic existence. The pollution, stress and poor sleep patterns don’t help much. Although many individuals realise the benefits of healthy regimes and precautions to maintain good health, critical illness can spring up suddenly. It is as important to remain financially secure in case this happens, as it is to try your best to be healthy in the first place. One of the solution to safeguard your financial condition when severe illness befalls you is to get critical illness cover. Critical illness Benefit cover comes as a policy or an add-on rider which you can opt along with your base life insurance policy by paying nominal extra cost or charge, that provides Lumpsum amount which can be used for treatment and care required due to critical illness. Illnesses that fall within the purview of such conditions include cancer, multiple sclerosis, permanent paralysis, certain heart diseases, kidney failure that warrants dialysis, and other conditions specified under the critical illness Benefit Rider Plan. The Rising Cost of Treatment The treatment of critical illnesses can run up huge costs, if you opt for treatment at famous private hospitals. New equipment and surgical methods are used in the treatment of many critical illnesses today, like robotic surgery, and these can increase your cost. Other expenses besides hospitalisation, include ongoing treatment and moving to another city for treatment etc. A critical illness may put you out of action where employment is concerned for a long period. This means a loss of income which has to be made up. Critical illness insurance can be of great help to you at such times. Such an insurance plan can be instrumental in taking away some of the stress of you and your family during tough times. While you may have a regular life insurance policy which covers hospitalization expenses, purchasing insurance that covers critical illness seems a necessity today. Irrespective of age or gender, you should consider buying a critical illness cover. Insurers pay policyholders a full lump sum amount (which is the sum assured under the plan), regardless of bills that are generated. The good part of a critical illness policy is that the lump sum amount is paid out to the insured as soon as a diagnosis of critical illness is made. The actual treatment cost may be lower than the payout, but this is no concern of the insurance company. The only clause laid down by such a policy is that the illness concerned should fall under the policy’s coverage and all other terms and conditions under the policy should be met. When the insured receives the lump sum from the insurer, they may use it for treatment of the critical illness, paying off home loan EMIs, daily expenditure, and any other expenses incurred due to
a loss of income due to severe illness. In any critical illness plan, there is normally a survival period which can be up to a month depending on the terms of the policy. This means that the policyholder must survive for up to a month after the diagnosis of a critical illness is made, to benefit from the policy. Nonetheless, before you head to purchase a critical illness insurance policy, it is important to evaluate the amount you wish to have coverage for. This sum should be the amount required to account for all the possible expenses you may have to incur in the future. Getting the Numbers Right To find out how much insurance coverage you require, you have to make an accurate calculation of your existing family income. You should also calculate the amount of expenses you incur to maintain your current lifestyle. Additionally, the cost of treatment, including consultations, surgery, medication, etc, should be considered. Assuming that an individual has calculated this total cost and it amounts to Rs. 40 lakhs today, since calculations have to account for the future, rates of inflation must factor in too. Critical illness cover must include the costs of rising inflation, so that total expenses under coverage don’t fall short of your financial requirements. Choose the Right Combination Critical illness insurance can be opted for as additional coverage with your present life insurance package. Furthermore, you can also buy it with a term insurance plan. When you do your homework, you will see that each of these combinations serves a different purpose, and depending on your requirements, you can opt for the coverage you need. Whatever you decide whether to go in for a standalone policy or to purchase it as rider with a base life insurance plan, the right combination should give you as extensive a coverage as you require. Conclusion Unfortunately, many Indians don’t choose to have critical illness insurance as they fear the high premiums involved. However, critical illness insurance is essential and can be one of the ways to tackle the huge costs of medical treatment. Nowadays, critical illness insurance is a must-have plan along with your basic life insurance policy. Consequently, you may remain stress-free as the potential financial worry is lifted off your shoulders, and you can avail the healthcare you need.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
